AMD.reasoning:
Agent count: 1 bullish (trend_momentum), 2 bearish (mean_reversion, catalyst_news), 2 neutral (breakout, macro_context). The headline read is bearish-leaning neutral. Critically, even the lone bullish agent (trend_momentum) does NOT recommend buying at current price — it explicitly requires a pullback to the 10 EMA (~316-320) before entry is valid. This means zero agents are recommending a long trade at the current price of $354.49. The convergence of 'no valid entry' across all 5 agents is the signal.

WEIGHT ALLOCATION FOR THIS CONTEXT: AMD is 3 trading days from a binary earnings catalyst (May 5, Q1 2026 after close). This is a catalyst-driven environment, giving swing_catalyst_news elevated weight. The macro backdrop is mixed-to-cautious with Iran escalation risk and Meta AI capex shock as live contagion vectors, giving swing_macro_context veto consideration. Result: catalyst_news and macro_context together dominate the decision frame.

EARNINGS VETO APPLIED: A stock in parabolic late-stage extension (RSI-14=83, Z-score=2.6, +74% in 21 days, price 52.6% above 50-SMA) entering a binary earnings event with analyst consensus targets below current price creates unfavorable risk geometry for either direction. The catalyst agent identifies a textbook 'buy the rumor, sell the news' setup; the macro agent independently calculates R/R at only 1.25-1.75:1 (below the required 2:1 minimum). No new position — long or short — is warranted.

THE PRODUCTIVE DISAGREEMENT: trend_momentum bullish + mean_reversion bearish on the same name is not a coin flip — it is a classic 'trend extended, reversal risk rising' read. AMD's daily trend is undeniably intact (ADX 74.94, EMA alignment clean, OBV rising), but the extension is parabolic (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83) with no reversal candle yet. This disagreement does not produce a tradable setup; it produces a waiting signal — trend intact but entry discipline requires a pullback. The mean_reversion agent's own rules disqualify the fade trade because the required reversal candle has not printed.

POST-EARNINGS PLAN: If AMD sells off into the $316-328 support cluster on earnings, the trend_momentum entry at $320 (10 EMA zone, Fib 23.6% confluence) becomes a high-conviction long setup with macro tailwind (AI capex cycle intact, wiki thesis validated). That scenario would produce a bullish signal at materially higher confidence. If AMD gaps up further on a beat, wait for the dust to settle and reassess overbought conditions before chasing.
AMD.key_conflicts: The sharpest conflict is between swing_trend_momentum (bullish) and swing_catalyst_news + swing_mean_reversion (bearish). Trend_momentum sees a pristine multi-timeframe uptrend — ADX 74.94, daily and hourly EMAs perfectly stacked, ROC accelerating to +74% over 21 days — and calls for a buy on pullback to the 10 EMA (~320). Mean_reversion counters that with Z-score 2.6, RSI-14 at 83.06, and price 52.6% above the 50-SMA, a statistical snap-back is the higher-probability outcome. Catalyst_news adds the most actionable layer: earnings in 3 days, consensus price targets ($274-290 average) are well below current price ($354), CEO Lisa Su sold $16M in March, and the street has already priced in the bull case — 'buy the rumor' has already played out. Breakout is neutral because the breakout already happened weeks ago (wide BB, no squeeze), and macro is neutral-to-negative because R/R is sub-2:1 and the binary event makes position sizing treacherous. The resolution: trend is real but the trade is untakeable at current price from any angle. Trend_momentum agrees — it too demands a pullback. That unexpected convergence on 'wait' is the signal.
